text: Hello, I'm sharing another laparoscopic cholecystectomy. This was another patient who had had a percutaneous cholecystostomy tube placed. In this particular case, it's because the patient had had COVID at the time of their cholecystitis that did not respond to antibiotic therapy. And because at the time we had a shutdown of elective surgeries, the patient had a cholecystostomy tube placed. and then came back for an elective cholecystectomy at a time that surgeries were reopened. Additionally, the patient was now COVID negative.
